# Design — Codex target + editor configurability

## R1 — Codex target
**Verified (Codex docs):** Codex reads `AGENTS.md` per directory; MCP is configured in `config.toml`, and can
be **project-scoped** via `.codex/config.toml` in trusted projects (`[mcp_servers.<id>]`, STDIO/HTTP).

- `src/config/schema.ts`: `targets: z.array(z.enum(["claude", "copilot", "codex"]))` (default unchanged).
- `src/generate/mcp.ts`: add `buildCodexMcp(ids): string` emitting TOML:
  ```toml
  # Codex MCP servers — generated by ai-workspace. See https://developers.openai.com/codex/mcp
  [mcp_servers.context7]
  command = "npx"
  args = ["-y", "@upstash/context7-mcp"]
  ```
  Reuses the shared MCP `REGISTRY`. A tiny, dependency-free TOML writer for our shape (command + args[] +
  optional env table); deterministic (stable key order). Empty server set ⇒ just the header comment.
- `src/generate/index.ts` (`generateWorkspace`): after the claude/copilot blocks, add
  ```ts
  if (config.targets.includes("codex"))
    add(writeFile(resolve(cwd, ".codex/config.toml"), buildCodexMcp(config.mcp)), t.desc.codexConfig);
  ```
  `AGENTS.md` is always written (the Codex adapter); no extra adapter file.

## R2 — `.vscode/` optional
- `src/config/schema.ts`: add `vscode: z.boolean().default(true)` (top-level).
- `src/generate/index.ts` (`generateWorkspace`):
  - Gate `buildVscodeMcpFile` (the `.vscode/mcp.json` write inside the copilot block) on `config.vscode`.
  - Gate the `generateVscode(cwd, wsConfig)` call (extensions + settings) on `config.vscode`.
- Default `true` ⇒ every `.vscode/*` path runs exactly as today (byte-identical).

## R3 — Wizard + AI skill + docs
- `src/commands/wizard.ts`: `WizardInputs.targets` and `SimpleBasics.targets` widen to include `"codex"`;
  add `vscode: boolean` to `WizardInputs`; `buildConfig` passes `targets` + `vscode`; `simpleDefaults` sets
  `vscode: true`.
- `src/commands/init.ts`:
  - `targets` multiselect (both Simple and Advanced) gains `{ value: "codex", label: "OpenAI Codex", hint:
    "AGENTS.md + .codex/config.toml" }`; cast widens to `(...|"codex")[]`.
  - Advanced: a `confirm` "Generate .vscode/ recommendations?" (default true) → `inputs.vscode`. Simple:
    `simpleDefaults` default true.
- `src/generate/guides.ts` (the `configure-workspace` skill body): add a line noting the Codex target and the
  `vscode` flag so the AI-guided path sets them.
- `src/i18n/strings.ts`: `desc.codexConfig` (done).
- Docs: `docs/project/USAGE.md` — a "Targets" note (claude/copilot/codex), the `vscode` flag, and the
  **Copilot in Visual Studio** toggle (Tools→Options→GitHub→Copilot→Copilot Chat → enable custom
  instructions). `DISTRIBUTION.md` unaffected.

## Why backward-compatible
- `targets` default `["claude","copilot"]` and `vscode` default `true` ⇒ existing configs render identically;
  AGENTS.md goldens untouched; second `sync` 0/0.
- Codex output is purely additive (new files only when `codex` is selected).

## Tests
- Codex: `targets:["codex"], vscode:false` → `AGENTS.md` + `.codex/config.toml` (TOML contains
  `[mcp_servers.context7]`), and **no** `CLAUDE.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, or `.vscode/`.
- Codex + context7 off (`mcp:[]`) → `.codex/config.toml` exists (header only).
- `vscode:false` on a normal config → no `.vscode/extensions.json`; default `true` → present.
- `buildConfig`: `vscode` flows through; targets accept codex.
- Idempotency: second `generate` 0 changed for a codex config.

## Risks / mitigations
- *Default drift* — defaults preserved; existing tests gate it.
- *TOML correctness* — keep the writer minimal and unit-cover the generated `.codex/config.toml` content.
